question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract call that exploits the fact that `props.putAll(properties)` allows user-defined properties to override the hardcoded `nodes.minNodeRewardBalance=Long.MAX_VALUE` guard, thereby enabling the EVM simulation to pay out staking rewards to system accounts and drain unpaid reward balances?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted `properties` map entry overriding `nodes.nodeRewardsEnabled=false` via the user-controlled `properties` field, causing the EVM simulation to skip the reward-payment guard and trigger incorrect staking reward disbursement during a simulated contract call?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a `nodes.minNodeRewardBalance=0` override into the `properties` map, which is merged last via `props.putAll(properties)`, causing the upstream consensus node library to treat the node reward balance threshold as zero and incorrectly pay out pending staking rewards during simulation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user override `executor.disableThrottles=false` through the user-defined `properties` map, causing throttle enforcement to interfere with the staking reward payment guard logic and allow reward theft during a simulated transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user override `contracts.chainId` via the user-defined `properties` map to a different chain ID, causing the EVM simulation to bypass chain-specific staking reward guards and trigger unintended reward disbursement?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getEvmVersionForBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a block number of -1 or Long.MIN_VALUE to `getEvmVersionForBlock()`, causing `floorEntry()` to return null and fall back to `EVM_VERSION` in a context where the staking reward guard is not initialized, leading to incorrect reward simulation?
